grantingOrganization: HK-dir (Directorate for Higher Education and Skills, Norway)
summary: The Nansen EDU programme supports partnerships between Norwegian and Ukrainian higher education institutions to strengthen Ukraine's educational capacity in priority fields. Projects must involve at least one Norwegian HEI and two Ukrainian HEIs, with funding up to NOK 5 million per project for up to 3 years. The programme emphasizes long-term cooperation, digital transformation, and contribution to Ukraine's reconstruction and recovery.
objectives: Strengthen Ukrainian institutions' capacity to deliver quality education
Improve access to qualified professionals in priority fields
Establish long-term cooperation between Norwegian and Ukrainian higher education partners
Support Ukraine's reconstruction and recovery through educational partnership
Build sustainable institutional capacity beyond project duration

requirements.description: Applicant must be a Norwegian higher education institution accredited by NOKUT
Norwegian applicant must offer bachelor's and master's programs
Project must include minimum 2 Ukrainian partner institutions
requirements.evidence: Letters of Commitment from Ukrainian partners

requirements.description: Ukrainian partners must be fully recognized and offer both bachelor's and master's level programs
European partners may participate supplementarily if contributing meaningfully
requirements.evidence: Letter of Commitment and justification of meaningful contribution

requirements.description: Project must focus on one or more thematic priorities: Digital transformation, Environment and energy, Physical and mental health, Law/social sciences/economics, Reconstruction and urban planning, STEM and agricultural sciences, or Teacher education
All projects must address anti-corruption measures for partners from countries with Corruption Perception Index ≤ 69
requirements.evidence: Description of preventive measures in project proposal

requirements.description: Projects must address climate and environment considerations (reduce carbon footprint)
requirements.evidence: Description of measures in project proposal

requirements.description: Gender equality dimension must be addressed in project design and implementation
requirements.evidence: Gender equality plan or description in project proposal

requirements.description: Human rights assessment must evaluate potential negative impacts on participation, accountability, non-discrimination, women's rights
requirements.evidence: Human rights impact assessment in project proposal

requirements.description: Must have ethical guidelines for prevention and follow-up of SEAH (sexual exploitation, abuse, and harassment)
Comprehensive risk assessment covering security, corruption, environmental/climate, social discrimination, data protection/IT security, academic freedom, and export control considerations
requirements.evidence: Risk assessment section in project proposal

requirements.description: Project Description using HK-dir template (PDF format)
requirements.evidence: Completed template uploaded to Espresso portal

requirements.description: Signed Declaration of Participation from applicant's legal representative
Letters of Commitment from all partners (excluding applicant)
requirements.evidence: Signed letters from each partner institution

requirements.description: Project Coordinator CV (maximum 4 pages)
Budget completed in Espresso portal
Application must be submitted in English through Espresso portal
Norwegian/non-Ukrainian partner personnel costs must not exceed 30% of total budget
Equipment must be purchased no later than 12 months before project end
Travel costs must follow Norwegian State Personnel Handbook rates
Projects must include budgeted activities in each year of the project period
All sub-grades in evaluation must be ≥ 4; at least two sub-grades must be ≥ 5 to qualify

evaluationCriteria.name: Relevance of the project
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of project need, alignment with call objectives, ambition level, innovation, and added value
keyIndicators: Need for the project and significance for target groups
Planned results are concrete and suitable to achieve call objectives
Innovation and/or complementarity to other measures/activities
Knowledge of existing initiatives; added value beyond what exists

evaluationCriteria.name: Quality of project design and implementation
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of implementation plan, risk management, and cost-effectiveness
keyIndicators: Concrete, comprehensive and realistic implementation plan
Quality of risk assessments, including cross-cutting issues and SEAH

evaluationCriteria.name: Quality of the project group and collaborative structures
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of consortium complementarity, expertise, task division, and interaction plans
keyIndicators: Complementarity of institutions/actors and overall expertise
Division of tasks shows commitment and active contribution from all partners
Clear and comprehensive plan for interaction between partners and stakeholders

evaluationCriteria.name: Project impact
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of potential impact, sustainability beyond project end, and evaluation mechanisms
keyIndicators: Potential impact for target groups, institutions, and actors in and outside the project
Quality of measures to ensure impacts are maintained after project end
Quality of measures to evaluate and document project impact

budget.totalAmount: NOK 80,000,000 total program budget; up to NOK 5,000,000 per project (~EUR 430,000)

categories.description: Salaries for project participants (release time or new hires), including all non-wage costs (holiday pay, insurance, indirect costs)
rules: Maximum 30% of total budget for Norwegian/non-Ukrainian partner personnel
Hourly rates must include all non-wage costs
Account category 5***

categories.description: External services necessary for project implementation
rules: Must be clearly justified as necessary
Account category 6***

categories.description: Physical items, licenses, software necessary for the project
rules: Must be purchased no later than 12 months before project end
Account category 4***

categories.description: Scholarships for Ukrainian students (up to 10 months)
rules: Bachelor's/Master's: EUR 250/month maximum
PhD: EUR 400/month maximum
Must be jointly awarded by Norwegian and Ukrainian partners
Account category 5***

categories.description: Project participant travel (domestic and international) and student exchange travel costs
rules: Must follow Norwegian State Personnel Handbook rates
Digital alternatives encouraged; climate-friendly transport preferred
Account category 7***

categories.description: Support for student and staff exchanges
rules: Student exchanges: up to 6 months per person
Staff exchanges: up to 30 days per stay (one or more stays)
Incoming students to Norway (>4 weeks): Norwegian State Educational Loan Fund rates
Incoming students (<4 weeks): government subsistence rates

categories.description: Costs not fitting other categories but necessary for implementation
rules: Must be justified
Account categories 6 and 7***

deliverables: Letters of commitment from all partners

timeline.duration: Between June 1 - July 1, 2026 to no later than June 30, 2029 (up to 3 years)
deliverables: Implementation according to approved project plan
Equipment procurement (no later than 12 months before project end)

targetBeneficiaries: Faculty and staff involved in educational partnerships
geographicScope: Norway-Ukraine bilateral cooperation with optional European partner participation
additionalNotes: This is NOT a mobility programme; physical exchange is secondary to educational partnership
War-affected and relocated Ukrainian institutions are particularly encouraged to participate
Programme is part of Norway's broader Nansen Support Programme for Ukraine
Projects should contribute to Ukraine's reconstruction and recovery efforts
Funder: Norwegian Ministry of Foreign Affairs
All applications must be submitted in English
Grading scale: 1-7, with qualification threshold requiring all sub-grades ≥ 4 and at least two sub-grades ≥ 5
Ukraine's Corruption Perception Index is below 69, triggering mandatory anti-corruption measures
Digital meetings and climate-friendly transport are strongly encouraged
Scholarship payments must be jointly awarded by Norwegian and Ukrainian partners
Export control and dual use considerations must be addressed in risk assessment
Academic freedom and national security interests must be considered
